signals.idea:
Sync Discord `_resources` channel posts into banodoco-website as auto-published resources, with a "Made with this" section containing reply media and a Discord-style comments thread.

# Context

Single shared Supabase project (`ujlwuvkrxlvoswwkerdf`) backs both brain-of-bndc (Discord archive bot, Python, Railway) and banodoco-website (React/Vite). Discord resource posts live in 5 forum channels: `resources` (1149372684220768367), `flux_resources` (1275200992136400967), `wan_resources` (1373291419434877078), `ltx_resources` (1457981813120176138), `acestep_resources` (1472633200491626526). Each is a forum, so every message has a `thread_id`; an OP is where `message_id == thread_id`. Archive pipeline already writes every message + reaction into `discord_messages`. Current volume: ~15 qualifying OPs in the last 30 days.

# Trigger + scope

- Thread OP in any of the 5 `_resources` channels with `reaction_count >= 5` (any emoji counts) becomes an `assets` row.
- Backfill: last 60 days on first run, chronological oldest→newest, resumable via per-channel cursor.
- Ongoing: polled every 10 min.
- No un-listing if reactions later drop.
- Auto-publish `admin_status='Listed'`; admin kill switch via `assets.is_hidden`.
- Each reply in the thread becomes an `asset_comments` row. Reply attachments become `media` rows linked via `asset_comment_media`. OP attachments go via existing `asset_media`.

# Schema deltas

Extend `assets`:
- `source` TEXT ('manual' | 'discord_import')
- `discord_thread_id` BIGINT UNIQUE NULL
- `discord_channel_id` BIGINT NULL
- `imported_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `last_synced_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `reactions_reached_threshold_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `is_hidden` BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE

New `asset_comments`:
- `id` UUID PK
- `asset_id` UUID FK → assets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- `discord_message_id` BIGINT UNIQUE NOT NULL
- `discord_thread_id` BIGINT NOT NULL
- `author_member_id` BIGINT FK → discord_members(member_id)
- `content` TEXT
- `reply_to_comment_id` UUID FK → asset_comments(id) NULL (resolves from Discord `reference_id`)
- `reply_to_discord_message_id` BIGINT NULL (denormalized for resolvability even when the parent isn't imported)
- `reaction_count` INT DEFAULT 0
- `discord_created_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `discord_edited_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `is_deleted` BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE
- `created_at`, `updated_at` TIMESTAMPTZ

New `asset_comment_media`:
- `comment_id` UUID FK → asset_comments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- `media_id` UUID FK → media(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- `sort_order` INT DEFAULT 0
- PK (comment_id, media_id)

`media` changes:
- Add `'discord-comment'` as a valid `classification` value
- Provenance stored in `media.metadata` JSONB: `{discord_message_id, discord_channel_id, discord_attachment_id, original_cdn_url, imported_at}`
- Dedup by `discord_attachment_id` (query `metadata->>'discord_attachment_id'`)

New `media_import_jobs` queue:
- `id` UUID PK
- `discord_attachment_id` BIGINT UNIQUE
- `discord_message_id` BIGINT
- `target_kind` TEXT ('asset_media' | 'asset_comment_media')
- `target_id` UUID (asset_id or comment_id depending on target_kind)
- `original_cdn_url` TEXT
- `filename` TEXT, `content_type` TEXT, `size_bytes` BIGINT
- `status` TEXT ('pending' | 'in_progress' | 'done' | 'failed' | 'skipped')
- `attempts` INT DEFAULT 0
- `last_error` TEXT
- `media_id` UUID FK → media(id) NULL (set on done)
- `created_at`, `updated_at`, `locked_until` TIMESTAMPTZ

Add indexes for: `assets.discord_thread_id`, `asset_comments(asset_id, discord_created_at)`, `asset_comments.discord_message_id`, `media_import_jobs(status, locked_until)`.

RLS on new tables: public read for `asset_comments` / `asset_comment_media` (matches `asset_media`); service-role only for `media_import_jobs`.

# Runtime

Two Supabase Edge Functions + pg_cron:

**`discord-resource-promoter`** (cron: every 10 min; ~30s wall clock):
1. For each of the 5 channels, advance the per-channel cursor (`discord_resource_sync_cursors` table: `channel_id`, `last_processed_created_at`, `last_backfill_cursor_at`, `backfill_complete` BOOL).
2. Discover qualifying thread OPs: `discord_messages` where `channel_id IN (...)`, `message_id = thread_id`, `reaction_count >= 5`, `created_at > cursor`.
3. Upsert `assets` rows keyed on `discord_thread_id`. Populate: `name` (first line of content, truncated), `description` (full content), `type` (infer via channel → 'lora' | 'tool'; default 'tool'), `member_id` (from author), `admin_status='Listed'`, `source='discord_import'`, `reactions_reached_threshold_at=NOW()`.
4. For each imported thread, fetch all replies: `discord_messages WHERE thread_id = X AND message_id != thread_id`. Upsert `asset_comments` keyed on `discord_message_id`. Resolve `reply_to_comment_id` from Discord `reference_id` where possible.
5. For every attachment (OP and replies), upsert a `media_import_jobs` row if not already present. Use Discord `attachment.id` as dedup key.
6. Update `assets.last_synced_at`.
7. Paginate/time-slice to stay under 30s budget; advance cursor even on partial progress.

**`discord-media-importer`** (cron: every 2 min; up to 400s):
1. Claim up to N pending jobs via `UPDATE ... SET status='in_progress', locked_until=NOW()+interval '10m' WHERE status='pending' AND (locked_until IS NULL OR locked_until < NOW()) ... RETURNING *`. Re-claim stuck `in_progress` past `locked_until`.
2. For each job:
   - Refresh Discord CDN URL (use bot token via Discord API `GET /channels/{channel_id}/messages/{message_id}` to get current signed URL — mirror logic from existing `refresh-media-urls` edge function).
   - Stream download from Discord CDN.
   - If video/gif: stream upload to Cloudflare Stream via tus; poll until `readyToStream`; capture `playback.hls`, `thumbnail`, `duration`.
   - If image: stream upload to Supabase Storage `user-uploads/discord-imports/{discord_message_id}/{attachment_id}-{filename}`; generate thumbnail via image transform if available.
   - Other (zip/json): upload to same bucket, no thumbnail.
   - Insert `media` row: `classification='discord-comment'` (or `'post'` for OP attachments — decide convention in plan), `member_id` from message author, Cloudflare/storage URLs populated, `metadata` with Discord provenance.
   - Insert junction row (`asset_media` or `asset_comment_media` based on `target_kind`).
   - Mark job `done`, set `media_id`.
3. On failure: increment `attempts`, write `last_error`, status back to `pending` with exponential backoff via `locked_until`. Skip permanently after 5 attempts (`status='failed'`).

Secrets required on Supabase Edge Function config:
- `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN`
- `CLOUDFLARE_STREAM_ACCOUNT_ID`
- `CLOUDFLARE_STREAM_API_TOKEN`
- Existing `SUPABASE_URL` / `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` implicit

# Frontend

Update `banodoco-website/src/pages/Resources/ResourceModal.tsx`:
1. Keep existing gallery strip (primary media + `asset_media`).
2. Add **"Made with this"** section below gallery — grid of all `asset_comment_media` thumbnails for the asset, newest first, click opens lightbox anchored to the parent comment.
3. Add **Discussion** section below — flat chronological list of `asset_comments`. Each row:
   - Author chip (avatar + global_name from `discord_members`) + timestamp
   - "Replying to @name" chip with quoted excerpt if `reply_to_comment_id` set
   - Markdown-rendered `content`
   - Attached media grid (inline video/image thumbs, click → lightbox)
   - Reaction count chip
   - "View on Discord" link (build URL from channel_id + message_id)
4. New hook `useAssetComments(assetId)` — single query joining `asset_comments` + `asset_comment_media` + `media` + `discord_members`, ordered by `discord_created_at`.
5. Resource card: small "From Discord" badge (channel name) on imported assets — subtle, not a big label.

# Observability + ops

- `media_import_jobs` table is the operational dashboard (count by status, oldest pending, failures with `last_error`).
- Errors logged to `system_logs` (existing infrastructure).
- Health check query: failed jobs in last 24h, stuck `in_progress` past lock, per-channel cursor age.
- Dry-run mode: env var `DRY_RUN=true` on promoter → logs would-be inserts without writing.

# Idempotency + edge cases to handle in plan

- Discord message edits: re-sync updates `asset_comments.content`, `discord_edited_at`.
- Discord message deletions: `discord_messages.is_deleted=true` → set `asset_comments.is_deleted=true` (UI hides).
- Attachment removal on edit: don't delete `media` (preserves history); could mark `is_deleted` on junction.
- Race: promoter tries to insert comment before OP asset exists — only process replies for threads whose asset row already exists.
- Rate limits: Discord API 50 req/s; space attachment URL refreshes.
- Cloudflare Stream failures: retry; skip after N attempts.
- `discord_members` might not yet have the author when a comment arrives — promoter should upsert a thin member row if missing (or the existing archive sync covers this — verify).
- Already-imported content: must be fully idempotent — reruns should produce zero new rows.

# Out of scope (do NOT include)

- Live-update (websocket) — polling is fine.
- Moderation UI beyond the `is_hidden` flag (no approval queue, no review surface).
- Cross-posting from website back to Discord.
- User-initiated imports ("turn this Discord post into a resource" button).
- Migrating existing manually-created `assets` rows — they stay `source='manual'`.
- Changing the archive pipeline on brain-of-bndc (the existing archive covers everything the promoter needs).

# Deliverables megaplan should produce

1. Ordered implementation phases with a clear first-working-version milestone (schema + promoter dry-run) before media rehosting is wired up.
2. Migration SQL file list (timestamped, in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/`).
3. Edge function directory structure under `supabase/functions/` (promoter, importer, shared helpers — there's already a `supabase/functions/_shared/` in the workspace).
4. Hook + component additions in `banodoco-website/src/` with concrete file paths.
5. pg_cron SQL snippets to register the two scheduled invocations.
6. Test/verification steps per phase (e.g. one-channel dry run, then rehost a single attachment, then full backfill).
7. Rollback plan per phase.
8. Explicit risk register (Cloudflare quota, Discord rate limits, edge function timeout, storage cost on 60-day backfill).

unresolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The plan still intentionally diverges from the approved runtime shape, and that remains a flag. The brief asked for two Supabase Edge Functions, a promoter directory under `supabase/functions/`, and a resumable cursor table; v3 still replaces the promoter with PL/pgSQL and removes the cursor table entirely. Documenting the deviation is useful, but it does not make it cease to be a deviation from the approved deliverables.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: There is still a smaller repo mismatch in the markdown guidance. v4 says `AssetDescription` should mirror `PostBodyRenderer.tsx`'s 'plugin + sanitization config', but `banodoco-website/src/components/posts/PostBodyRenderer.tsx:22-50` just hands text segments to `MarkdownTextSegment`, and `banodoco-website/src/components/posts/MarkdownRenderer.tsx:122-126` uses `react-markdown` with `remarkGfm` only; there is no separate sanitize/rehype config in this path today. The implementation note should point to the real `MarkdownRenderer` reuse path instead of a nonexistent sanitization layer.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: I also checked the actual data callers for Discord jump links. The repository already has guild-aware URL building in `brain-of-bndc/src/common/urls.py:7-12`, and the archive layer writes `guild_id` onto Discord rows, so v4’s plan to hard-code a single `GUILD_ID` constant in the frontend is still weaker than the data already available to callers. The safer caller contract is to pass through stored `guild_id` rather than invent a constant.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The plan still skips over existing guild-aware infrastructure when building Discord jump links. This codebase already persists `guild_id` on `discord_messages` and `discord_channels` and indexes it (`supabase/migrations/20260320000000_multi_server.sql:140-145`), but Step 21 still proposes a hard-coded frontend `GUILD_ID` constant instead of selecting the real guild from data. That is a brittle supporting-infrastructure mismatch rather than a necessity imposed by the repository.

Discord jump links: v4 still hard-codes a single frontend `GUILD_ID` even though the repository already stores and indexes `guild_id` on Discord data and has an existing helper that builds jump URLs from real `guild_id`/`thread_id` values.

resolved_flags.concern: Discord resource promotion: late-threshold coverage is still incomplete because the revised rolling 60-day scan misses threads older than 60 days that reach the reaction threshold later.
resolved_flags.resolution: v3 commits to concrete single-option decisions for every v2 flag. Discovery drops the 60-day window (anti-join + reaction threshold are sufficient; any qualifying OP imports regardless of age, fixing FLAG-001/correctness-1). Promoter uses `WHERE EXISTS` prefilter on member existence plus a parallel `system_logs` insert naming skipped rows, so a single FK violation no longer aborts the sync (fixes FLAG-006/correctness-2). The Cloudflare duration promise is dropped — the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` only writes HLS URL + thumbnail + storage_provider per lines 210-240, and v3's contract matches that exactly (fixes FLAG-005/all_locations-1). The vault bootstrap is replaced with a self-contained `internal.secrets` table + SECURITY DEFINER accessor migration that applies cleanly on `supabase db reset` without any pre-existing secret; the operator seeds the key via a documented one-line INSERT at provisioning time (fixes all_locations-2). The "existing lightbox" promise for comment media is downgraded to `<a href={media.url} target="_blank">` tiles; a real lightbox is explicitly flagged as follow-up (fixes FLAG-007/scope/callers). The PL/pgSQL pivot from the brief's two-edge-function shape is promoted to an explicit settled decision in the Overview with rationale (addresses issue_hints). Adds a concrete regexp validation step and a missing-member regression test to the Validation Order.

resolved_flags.concern: Discord member fallback: the proposed thin upsert is incompatible with the current members schema because username is still required.
resolved_flags.resolution: Reworked v1 to address all 17 gate-identified defects. Largest structural changes: (1) promoter becomes a PL/pgSQL stored function invoked by pg_cron directly, matching the repo's existing `schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql` pattern and eliminating the edge-function auth problem; (2) discovery uses an anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id` within a rolling 60-day window instead of a `created_at > cursor` scan, so late-threshold crossings are imported; (3) media importer reuses the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` + `cloudflare-stream-backfill` functions (no Cloudflare polling); (4) frontend work moves to `ResourceDetail/index.tsx` (the live surface) with matching changes at `ResourceModal.tsx` to honor the user's scope note; (5) asset `type` mapping fixed to `workflow`/`lora` matching `SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`; (6) member fallback removed — trust archive pipeline; (7) `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all updated to SELECT + filter on `is_hidden`; (8) `asset_comment_media` + `asset_media` gain `is_deleted` columns for attachment-removal-on-edit; (9) vault bootstrap for service-role key is a first-class migration step; (10) shared `AssetDescription` component applies markdown rendering to both manual and Discord-imported asset descriptions with an explicit success criterion, no custom embed tokens.

resolved_flags.concern: Resource typing: importing assets as `tool` conflicts with the website’s existing `lora`/`workflow` assumptions.
resolved_flags.resolution: Reworked v1 to address all 17 gate-identified defects. Largest structural changes: (1) promoter becomes a PL/pgSQL stored function invoked by pg_cron directly, matching the repo's existing `schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql` pattern and eliminating the edge-function auth problem; (2) discovery uses an anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id` within a rolling 60-day window instead of a `created_at > cursor` scan, so late-threshold crossings are imported; (3) media importer reuses the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` + `cloudflare-stream-backfill` functions (no Cloudflare polling); (4) frontend work moves to `ResourceDetail/index.tsx` (the live surface) with matching changes at `ResourceModal.tsx` to honor the user's scope note; (5) asset `type` mapping fixed to `workflow`/`lora` matching `SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`; (6) member fallback removed — trust archive pipeline; (7) `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all updated to SELECT + filter on `is_hidden`; (8) `asset_comment_media` + `asset_media` gain `is_deleted` columns for attachment-removal-on-edit; (9) vault bootstrap for service-role key is a first-class migration step; (10) shared `AssetDescription` component applies markdown rendering to both manual and Discord-imported asset descriptions with an explicit success criterion, no custom embed tokens.

resolved_flags.concern: Resource frontend surface: the plan targets an effectively unused modal and does not cover the route-driven ResourceDetail page that users actually hit.

Largest structural changes: (1) promoter becomes a PL/pgSQL stored function invoked by pg_cron directly, matching the repo's existing `schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql` pattern and eliminating the edge-function auth problem; (2) discovery uses an anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id` within a rolling 60-day window instead of a `created_at > cursor` scan, so late-threshold crossings are imported; (3) media importer reuses the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` + `cloudflare-stream-backfill` functions (no Cloudflare polling); (4) frontend work moves to `ResourceDetail/index.tsx` (the live surface) with matching changes at `ResourceModal.tsx` to honor the user's scope note; (5) asset `type` mapping fixed to `workflow`/`lora` matching `SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`; (6) member fallback removed — trust archive pipeline; (7) `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all updated to SELECT + filter on `is_hidden`; (8) `asset_comment_media` + `asset_media` gain `is_deleted` columns for attachment-removal-on-edit; (9) vault bootstrap for service-role key is a first-class migration step; (10) shared `AssetDescription` component applies markdown rendering to both manual and Discord-imported asset descriptions with an explicit success criterion, no custom embed tokens.

resolved_flags.concern: Discord media sync infrastructure: the revised Cloudflare/vault reuse story is still incomplete against the actual repository contracts.
resolved_flags.resolution: v3 commits to concrete single-option decisions for every v2 flag. Discovery drops the 60-day window (anti-join + reaction threshold are sufficient; any qualifying OP imports regardless of age, fixing FLAG-001/correctness-1). Promoter uses `WHERE EXISTS` prefilter on member existence plus a parallel `system_logs` insert naming skipped rows, so a single FK violation no longer aborts the sync (fixes FLAG-006/correctness-2). The Cloudflare duration promise is dropped — the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` only writes HLS URL + thumbnail + storage_provider per lines 210-240, and v3's contract matches that exactly (fixes FLAG-005/all_locations-1). The vault bootstrap is replaced with a self-contained `internal.secrets` table + SECURITY DEFINER accessor migration that applies cleanly on `supabase db reset` without any pre-existing secret; the operator seeds the key via a documented one-line INSERT at provisioning time (fixes all_locations-2). The "existing lightbox" promise for comment media is downgraded to `<a href={media.url} target="_blank">` tiles; a real lightbox is explicitly flagged as follow-up (fixes FLAG-007/scope/callers). The PL/pgSQL pivot from the brief's two-edge-function shape is promoted to an explicit settled decision in the Overview with rationale (addresses issue_hints). Adds a concrete regexp validation step and a missing-member regression test to the Validation Order.

resolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The revision also drops the requested first-working-version milestone of 'schema + promoter dry-run' before media rehosting. The brief explicitly calls for promoter dry-run behavior, and the repo has multiple existing dry-run patterns in scripts, but v3 has no promoter preview mode at all and jumps straight to live `SELECT internal.discord_promote_resources()` validation.
resolved_flags.resolution: v4 applies four narrow mechanical corrections to v3 without changing any settled architectural decision. (1) The asset and comment INSERTs now use `WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM assets/asset_comments ...)` plus `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, and drift is handled by a separate conditional UPDATE that only touches rows where `description IS DISTINCT FROM m.content` — so `GET DIAGNOSTICS ROW_COUNT` is genuinely zero on idempotent reruns (fixes FLAG-009/correctness-1). (2) Every `system_logs` INSERT now uses the real column names `logger_name, level, message, extra` per `supabase/migrations/20251210000000_create_system_logs.sql:5-18` (fixes FLAG-008/correctness-2). (3) A new Phase 1 migration adds the partial composite index `CREATE INDEX discord_messages_resource_op_idx ON discord_messages (channel_id, reaction_count DESC) WHERE message_id = thread_id AND is_deleted = FALSE`, matching the all-history scan's predicates exactly (fixes FLAG-010/all_locations). (4) The promoter function gains a `dry_run BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE` parameter; when TRUE it computes intended-insert counts via SELECT, writes a `level='info'` summary row with `extra->>'dry_run'='true'`, emits `RAISE NOTICE`, and returns without mutating. Execution Order's new first-working-version milestone is `SELECT internal.discord_promote_resources(dry_run := TRUE)` before any live run (fixes issue_hints-2). The PL/pgSQL pivot + cursor-removal remains as a gate-endorsed settled decision cited once in the Overview (closes issue_hints-1/issue_hints by acknowledgment — this has been endorsed twice and the plan does not re-open it).

resolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The main promoter insert still does not implement the anti-join behavior the overview claims. In Step 7, the valid asset insert selects every qualifying OP with an existing member and relies on `ON CONFLICT (discord_thread_id) DO UPDATE`; because there is no `NOT EXISTS` filter on the insert path, every rerun will re-update already imported assets and `GET DIAGNOSTICS v_a = ROW_COUNT` will not stay at zero, which conflicts with the plan’s own idempotency/counting claims.
resolved_flags.resolution: v4 applies four narrow mechanical corrections to v3 without changing any settled architectural decision. (1) The asset and comment INSERTs now use `WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM assets/asset_comments ...)` plus `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, and drift is handled by a separate conditional UPDATE that only touches rows where `description IS DISTINCT FROM m.content` — so `GET DIAGNOSTICS ROW_COUNT` is genuinely zero on idempotent reruns (fixes FLAG-009/correctness-1). (2) Every `system_logs` INSERT now uses the real column names `logger_name, level, message, extra` per `supabase/migrations/20251210000000_create_system_logs.sql:5-18` (fixes FLAG-008/correctness-2). (3) A new Phase 1 migration adds the partial composite index `CREATE INDEX discord_messages_resource_op_idx ON discord_messages (channel_id, reaction_count DESC) WHERE message_id = thread_id AND is_deleted = FALSE`, matching the all-history scan's predicates exactly (fixes FLAG-010/all_locations). (4) The promoter function gains a `dry_run BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE` parameter; when TRUE it computes intended-insert counts via SELECT, writes a `level='info'` summary row with `extra->>'dry_run'='true'`, emits `RAISE NOTICE`, and returns without mutating. Execution Order's new first-working-version milestone is `SELECT internal.discord_promote_resources(dry_run := TRUE)` before any live run (fixes issue_hints-2). The PL/pgSQL pivot + cursor-removal remains as a gate-endorsed settled decision cited once in the Overview (closes issue_hints-1/issue_hints by acknowledgment — this has been endorsed twice and the plan does not re-open it).

resolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The observability SQL in the plan is still mismatched to the actual schema. `supabase/migrations/20251210000000_create_system_logs.sql:5-18` defines `system_logs` with `logger_name` and `extra` columns, not `component` and `payload`, yet v3 still proposes inserts like `INSERT INTO public.system_logs (component, level, message, payload) ...`. As written, those logging statements would fail until the column names are corrected.

resolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The proposed asset type mapping (`lora` or `tool`) does not match the website’s current resource model. Manual resource creation only offers `lora` and `workflow` (`banodoco-website/src/pages/SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`), and the cards/filters branch on `asset.type === 'workflow'` (`ResourceModal.tsx:151-156`, `ResourceCard.tsx:151-154`, `useResourceFilters.ts:159-160`). Importing Discord rows with `type='tool'` would render them as LoRAs in some places and make them unfilterable in others.

Adds a concrete regexp validation step and a missing-member regression test to the Validation Order.

resolved_flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The plan scopes the frontend work around `ResourceModal`, but `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` only finds the component definition and no callers. The live resource surface is `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx`, which still renders `resource.description` as plain text at lines 166-169 and already owns the visible 'Made with this' gallery. Limiting the change to the dead modal leaves the actual resource page behavior unchanged.
resolved_flags.resolution: Reworked v1 to address all 17 gate-identified defects. Largest structural changes: (1) promoter becomes a PL/pgSQL stored function invoked by pg_cron directly, matching the repo's existing `schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql` pattern and eliminating the edge-function auth problem; (2) discovery uses an anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id` within a rolling 60-day window instead of a `created_at > cursor` scan, so late-threshold crossings are imported; (3) media importer reuses the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` + `cloudflare-stream-backfill` functions (no Cloudflare polling); (4) frontend work moves to `ResourceDetail/index.tsx` (the live surface) with matching changes at `ResourceModal.tsx` to honor the user's scope note; (5) asset `type` mapping fixed to `workflow`/`lora` matching `SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`; (6) member fallback removed — trust archive pipeline; (7) `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all updated to SELECT + filter on `is_hidden`; (8) `asset_comment_media` + `asset_media` gain `is_deleted` columns for attachment-removal-on-edit; (9) vault bootstrap for service-role key is a first-class migration step; (10) shared `AssetDescription` component applies markdown rendering to both manual and Discord-imported asset descriptions with an explicit success criterion, no custom embed tokens.

resolved_flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The broader 'resource visibility' surface is larger than the plan accounts for. Resource lists and profile counts currently come from `useResources.ts`, `useCommunityResources.ts`, `useCommunityResource.ts`, and `useUserProfile.ts`, all of which filter only on `admin_status` and never consider a future `assets.is_hidden` kill switch (`useResources.ts:51-74`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-127`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-113`, `useUserProfile.ts:125-133`). If `is_hidden` is meant to hide imported assets globally, the change needs to reach those callers too, not just one modal/card.

resolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The Cloudflare reuse story is still incomplete against the actual repository code. `supabase/functions/cloudflare-stream-webhook/index.ts:210-240` only writes `cloudflare_playback_hls_url`, `cloudflare_thumbnail_url`, and `storage_provider`; it does not persist duration, even though the revised plan and success criteria say the existing webhook will populate HLS, thumbnail, and duration for imported videos. That supporting-infrastructure contract is still inaccurate unless the plan adds a webhook change or drops duration from the promise.
resolved_flags.resolution: v3 commits to concrete single-option decisions for every v2 flag. Discovery drops the 60-day window (anti-join + reaction threshold are sufficient; any qualifying OP imports regardless of age, fixing FLAG-001/correctness-1). Promoter uses `WHERE EXISTS` prefilter on member existence plus a parallel `system_logs` insert naming skipped rows, so a single FK violation no longer aborts the sync (fixes FLAG-006/correctness-2). The Cloudflare duration promise is dropped — the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` only writes HLS URL + thumbnail + storage_provider per lines 210-240, and v3's contract matches that exactly (fixes FLAG-005/all_locations-1). The vault bootstrap is replaced with a self-contained `internal.secrets` table + SECURITY DEFINER accessor migration that applies cleanly on `supabase db reset` without any pre-existing secret; the operator seeds the key via a documented one-line INSERT at provisioning time (fixes all_locations-2). The "existing lightbox" promise for comment media is downgraded to `<a href={media.url} target="_blank">` tiles; a real lightbox is explicitly flagged as follow-up (fixes FLAG-007/scope/callers). The PL/pgSQL pivot from the brief's two-edge-function shape is promoted to an explicit settled decision in the Overview with rationale (addresses issue_hints). Adds a concrete regexp validation step and a missing-member regression test to the Validation Order.

resolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The new vault bootstrap step is not yet self-contained enough to satisfy the plan’s own `supabase db reset` criterion. There is no existing `vault.create_secret`, `internal.get_service_role_key`, or secret-seeding pattern anywhere under `supabase/`, and the proposed migration depends on an out-of-band `current_setting('app.settings.service_role_key_value', true)` value that the repo never sets. As written, the bootstrap is still an infrastructure gap rather than a ready migration recipe.

resolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: I traced the current callers that consume resource rows, and they all pass through the existing hooks with narrow assumptions about the payload: `useResources.ts` reads `id, type, name, description, admin_status, ...`, `useCommunityResources.ts` reads `id, name, description, type, lora_link, ...`, and `useCommunityResource.ts` reads `id, name, description, type, ...` (`useResources.ts:51-65`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-124`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-109`). None of those callers request `source`, `discord_channel_id`, or `is_hidden`, so the downstream badge, jump-link, and hide logic proposed in the plan would have no inputs unless the callers themselves are revised.

resolved_flags.concern: Discord sync logging: the revised plan still targets nonexistent `system_logs` columns, so the proposed promoter/importer logging SQL would fail against the actual schema.
resolved_flags.resolution: v4 applies four narrow mechanical corrections to v3 without changing any settled architectural decision. (1) The asset and comment INSERTs now use `WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM assets/asset_comments ...)` plus `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, and drift is handled by a separate conditional UPDATE that only touches rows where `description IS DISTINCT FROM m.content` — so `GET DIAGNOSTICS ROW_COUNT` is genuinely zero on idempotent reruns (fixes FLAG-009/correctness-1). (2) Every `system_logs` INSERT now uses the real column names `logger_name, level, message, extra` per `supabase/migrations/20251210000000_create_system_logs.sql:5-18` (fixes FLAG-008/correctness-2). (3) A new Phase 1 migration adds the partial composite index `CREATE INDEX discord_messages_resource_op_idx ON discord_messages (channel_id, reaction_count DESC) WHERE message_id = thread_id AND is_deleted = FALSE`, matching the all-history scan's predicates exactly (fixes FLAG-010/all_locations). (4) The promoter function gains a `dry_run BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE` parameter; when TRUE it computes intended-insert counts via SELECT, writes a `level='info'` summary row with `extra->>'dry_run'='true'`, emits `RAISE NOTICE`, and returns without mutating. Execution Order's new first-working-version milestone is `SELECT internal.discord_promote_resources(dry_run := TRUE)` before any live run (fixes issue_hints-2). The PL/pgSQL pivot + cursor-removal remains as a gate-endorsed settled decision cited once in the Overview (closes issue_hints-1/issue_hints by acknowledgment — this has been endorsed twice and the plan does not re-open it).

resolved_flags.concern: Discord promoter idempotency: the revised main insert path still lacks the claimed anti-join, so reruns will update every already-imported qualifying OP and its row-count/idempotency claims will not hold as written.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 4. Weighted score trajectory: 23.25 -> 20.75 -> 8.5 -> 7.25. Plan deltas: 91.5%, 56.0%, 43.9%. Recurring critiques: 3. Resolved flags: 22. Open significant flags: 5.

items.criterion: All Phase 1 migrations apply cleanly via `supabase db reset` on a scratch branch, including `internal.secrets` (with no pre-existing value) and the new partial index on `discord_messages`
`assets` gains source, discord_thread_id, discord_channel_id, imported_at, last_synced_at, reactions_reached_threshold_at, is_hidden plus unique partial index on discord_thread_id
`asset_comments`, `asset_comment_media` (with is_deleted), `media_import_jobs` exist with specified columns, indexes, and FKs to `members(member_id)`
`asset_media` gains is_deleted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE

RLS enabled on all new tables; asset_comments / asset_comment_media have public-read SELECT filtered by is_deleted=false; media_import_jobs and internal.secrets have no policies (service-role / postgres only)

`internal.secrets` is postgres-owned with RLS enabled; `internal.get_service_role_key()` is SECURITY DEFINER with EXECUTE granted only to postgres; migration applies cleanly without a pre-seeded secret

Partial composite index `discord_messages_resource_op_idx ON discord_messages (channel_id, reaction_count DESC) WHERE message_id = thread_id AND is_deleted = FALSE` exists and is used by the promoter's discovery query (confirmed via EXPLAIN ANALYZE)
`internal.discord_promote_resources(dry_run BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE)` exists, is SECURITY DEFINER, and is callable via pg_cron without HTTP

Dry-run invocation `SELECT * FROM internal.discord_promote_resources(dry_run := TRUE)` returns the expected counts, writes a single `system_logs` row per channel with `logger_name='discord_resource_promoter'` and `level='info'` and `extra->>'dry_run'='true'`, and performs zero writes to assets/asset_comments/media_import_jobs

All `system_logs` INSERTs in promoter and importer use the real columns `logger_name, level, message, extra` (matching `supabase/migrations/20251210000000_create_system_logs.sql:5-18`); no reference to `component` or `payload`
Promoter discovery has no `created_at` time window; any qualifying OP of any age imports on the next tick
Main asset INSERT includes `AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM assets a WHERE a.discord_thread_id = m.message_id)` so that `assets_inserted = 0` on clean reruns; same pattern for the comments INSERT
Description/name drift is handled by a separate UPDATE statement conditional on `IS DISTINCT FROM`, so `assets_updated = 0` when no content changed
Running the promoter a second time produces zero net new rows AND zero UPDATEs (`assets_inserted = assets_updated = comments_inserted = comments_updated = jobs_enqueued = 0`)
An OP older than 60 days manually updated to `reaction_count=5` is imported by the next promoter tick

Promoter uses `WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM members ...)` prefilter on asset + comment inserts; missing-member rows are logged to `system_logs` with `logger_name='discord_resource_promoter'` and `level='warning'`
With a manually-deleted referenced `members` row, the promoter completes successfully, the skipped OP is logged, and all other OPs import
Promoter maps channel 1149372684220768367 -> type='workflow' and the other four channels -> type='lora'
`regexp_match(url, '/attachments/\d+/(\d+)/')` correctly extracts the attachment ID from a Discord CDN URL with `?ex=…&is=…&hm=…` query parameters
A Discord edit that removes an attachment marks the corresponding junction row `is_deleted=TRUE` on the next promoter tick
A Discord message edit updates `asset_comments.content` and `discord_edited_at`; a deletion sets `asset_comments.is_deleted=true`
Media importer processes a single seeded job end-to-end: creates a `media` row (Stream UID for video; Storage URL for image), creates the junction row, marks the job `status='done'` with `media_id`
Video importer does NOT poll Cloudflare; existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` populates `cloudflare_playback_hls_url` + `cloudflare_thumbnail_url` when ready; duration is intentionally not promised
Rehosted video URLs return a valid HLS playlist (HTTP 200, `#EXTM3U` in body); rehosted image URLs return HTTP 200 with image content-type
A simulated failed job increments `attempts`, records `last_error`, is re-queued with growing `locked_until`, and moves to `status='failed'` after 5 attempts

pg_cron has two schedules: `discord-resource-promoter` (*/10, direct SQL call to `internal.discord_promote_resources()`, no args so dry_run=FALSE) and `discord-media-importer` (*/2, `net.http_post` with Authorization via `internal.get_service_role_key()`)
`useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all SELECT source, discord_channel_id, is_hidden and filter is_hidden=false
Shared `AssetDescription` component renders `asset.description` with react-markdown + remark-gfm mirroring `PostBodyRenderer.tsx`, no custom embed tokens
`ResourceDetail/index.tsx:166-169` renders description via `AssetDescription` for both manual and Discord-imported assets
`ResourceModal.tsx:186-189` renders description via `AssetDescription`
ResourceDetail shows 'Made with this' grid, 'Discussion' thread, and 'From Discord' badge only when `resource.source === 'discord_import'`; manual assets render unchanged
Comment media and 'Made with this' tiles are anchor tags with `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"` pointing at the rehosted URL; no in-app lightbox in v1
Each rendered comment shows author avatar + name, markdown content via `AssetDescription`, reply chip when applicable, attachment tiles, reaction count, and working 'View on Discord' link
`useAssetComments` fetches joined data in one Supabase query, filters is_deleted=false on both layers, orders by `discord_created_at` ascending
Setting `assets.is_hidden=true` on an imported asset makes it disappear from all four hook-backed surfaces and from `ResourceDetail` by URL
Existing website tests and Supabase type generation still pass after schema additions
Resource cards show a subtle 'From Discord' badge with the channel friendly name for imported assets
Media importer wall-clock stays under 400s per invocation; promoter (SQL) finishes in under 10s per run
Health-check SQL returns usable output (stuck jobs, 24h failures, staleness, recent promoter/importer system_logs)
Rollback + follow-up doc exists and explicitly names deferred items (comment lightbox, Cloudflare duration webhook extension, supabase_vault migration)
No Cloudflare Stream quota / storage cost surprises during backfill

unresolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The plan still intentionally diverges from the approved runtime shape, and that remains a flag. The brief asked for two Supabase Edge Functions, a promoter directory under `supabase/functions/`, and a resumable cursor table; v3 still replaces the promoter with PL/pgSQL and removes the cursor table entirely. Documenting the deviation is useful, but it does not make it cease to be a deviation from the approved deliverables.
unresolved_flags.evidence: v4 applies four narrow mechanical corrections to v3 without changing any settled architectural decision. (1) The asset and comment INSERTs now use `WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM assets/asset_comments ...)` plus `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, and drift is handled by a separate conditional UPDATE that only touches rows where `description IS DISTINCT FROM m.content` — so `GET DIAGNOSTICS ROW_COUNT` is genuinely zero on idempotent reruns (fixes FLAG-009/correctness-1). (2) Every `system_logs` INSERT now uses the real column names `logger_name, level, message, extra` per `supabase/migrations/20251210000000_create_system_logs.sql:5-18` (fixes FLAG-008/correctness-2). (3) A new Phase 1 migration adds the partial composite index `CREATE INDEX discord_messages_resource_op_idx ON discord_messages (channel_id, reaction_count DESC) WHERE message_id = thread_id AND is_deleted = FALSE`, matching the all-history scan's predicates exactly (fixes FLAG-010/all_locations). (4) The promoter function gains a `dry_run BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE` parameter; when TRUE it computes intended-insert counts via SELECT, writes a `level='info'` summary row with `extra->>'dry_run'='true'`, emits `RAISE NOTICE`, and returns without mutating. Execution Order's new first-working-version milestone is `SELECT internal.discord_promote_resources(dry_run := TRUE)` before any live run (fixes issue_hints-2). The PL/pgSQL pivot + cursor-removal remains as a gate-endorsed settled decision cited once in the Overview (closes issue_hints-1/issue_hints by acknowledgment — this has been endorsed twice and the plan does not re-open it).

unresolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: There is still a smaller repo mismatch in the markdown guidance. v4 says `AssetDescription` should mirror `PostBodyRenderer.tsx`'s 'plugin + sanitization config', but `banodoco-website/src/components/posts/PostBodyRenderer.tsx:22-50` just hands text segments to `MarkdownTextSegment`, and `banodoco-website/src/components/posts/MarkdownRenderer.tsx:122-126` uses `react-markdown` with `remarkGfm` only; there is no separate sanitize/rehype config in this path today. The implementation note should point to the real `MarkdownRenderer` reuse path instead of a nonexistent sanitization layer.

unresolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: I also checked the actual data callers for Discord jump links. The repository already has guild-aware URL building in `brain-of-bndc/src/common/urls.py:7-12`, and the archive layer writes `guild_id` onto Discord rows, so v4’s plan to hard-code a single `GUILD_ID` constant in the frontend is still weaker than the data already available to callers. The safer caller contract is to pass through stored `guild_id` rather than invent a constant.

unresolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The plan still skips over existing guild-aware infrastructure when building Discord jump links. This codebase already persists `guild_id` on `discord_messages` and `discord_channels` and indexes it (`supabase/migrations/20260320000000_multi_server.sql:140-145`), but Step 21 still proposes a hard-coded frontend `GUILD_ID` constant instead of selecting the real guild from data. That is a brittle supporting-infrastructure mismatch rather than a necessity imposed by the repository.

unresolved_flags.concern: Discord jump links: v4 still hard-codes a single frontend `GUILD_ID` even though the repository already stores and indexes `guild_id` on Discord data and has an existing helper that builds jump URLs from real `guild_id`/`thread_id` values.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `supabase/migrations/20260320000000_multi_server.sql:140-145` adds guild_id indexes, the archive writes guild_id onto messages/channels, and `brain-of-bndc/src/common/urls.py:7-12` already constructs jump URLs from passed `guild_id`, `channel_id`, `message_id`, and `thread_id`.
